column2: The depressing thing about tennis is that no matter how good I get, I'll never be as good as a wall. — Mitch Hedberg
Actually, I tossed it nicely, landed nicely, like airplane. No warning, beautiful. That's the art of throwing racquets. — Goran Ivanišević
I played like Kournikova — 20 double faults per match. — Goran Ivanišević
I was pretty nice to the racket all last week. I was surprised how easy it broke. Maybe there is something wrong with it, or I'm too strong. — Goran Ivanišević
To err is human. To put the blame on someone else is doubles. — Anonymous
In tennis, the addict moves about a hard rectangle and seeks to ambush a fuzzy ball with a modified snow-shoe. — Elliot Chaze
I'd love to hate you but you are really nice. — Andy Roddick on Federer
I will follow that man around the world, the first thing he will see when he turns around is my shadow. — Jimmy Connors on Borg
My ass has got more personality than Ivan Lendl. — John McEnroe on Lendl
They looked like one of my sweatshirts after they've got wrinkled because of all the travel. — John McEnroe on sleeveless shirts
Tennis is a funny game; unbelievable highs and the lows are just as low. — John McEnroe
I had a feeling today that Venus Williams would either win or lose. — Martina Navratilova
I'd rather be No. 2 in Chile and No. 1 in the world. — Nicolás Massú
You cannot be serious! — John McEnroe
Tennis is an addiction that no one wants to cure.
I play tennis because punching people is frowned upon.
Tennis: The only sport where you can gain weight while playing.
If you don’t get nervous, you’re not paying attention.
The scoreboard doesn’t reflect effort—it reflects execution.
Tennis is like dating—if you don’t keep up, you’ll get dumped.
Champions keep playing until they get it right. — Billie Jean King
I’m a very positive thinker, and I think that is what helps me the most in difficult moments. — Roger Federer
A champion is defined not by their wins but by how they can recover when they fall. — Serena Williams
I play each point like my life depends on it. — Rafael Nadal
Just believe in yourself. Even if you don’t, pretend that you do and, at some point, you will. — Venus Williams
It’s one-on-one out there, man. There ain’t no hiding. I can’t pass the ball. — Pete Sampras
Nothing can substitute for just plain hard work. — Andre Agassi
Success is a journey, not a destination. The doing is often more important than the outcome. — Arthur Ashe
One important key to success is self-confidence. An important key to self-confidence is preparation. — Arthur Ashe
Tennis is a mental game. Everyone is fit, everyone hits great forehands and backhands. — Novak Djokovic
Tennis is the loneliest sport. — Andre Agassi
Tennis is mostly mental. Of course, you must have a lot of physical skill, but you can’t play tennis well and not be a good thinker. — Venus Williams
Tennis is not a gentle game. Psychologically, it is vicious. — Richard Evans
The microphone is open. There is no delay. If I see something and want to say it right now, I can. — Pam Shriver
If I lose early in the tournament, I can work out and try to improve. When the tournament is done, I'm just trying to get well and work on the recovery for the next day. — Caroline Garcia
Do you have any rules in there? Stop showing me your hand, ok? Don’t tell me to be quiet ok, when I wanna talk, I’ll talk alright? ... He takes ten seconds. — Player venting
I’m an actress, model and athlete, and I’d put athlete third on the list. — Serena Williams
I’m really exciting. I smile a lot, I win a lot, and I’m really sexy. — Serena Williams
Love: the only score that sounds like you’re winning when you’re losing.
My strategy? Serve it, smack it, wait for the chaos.
If tennis was easy, they'd call it ‘matcha’ – because every point is a daily grind.
I don’t always lose focus—but when I do, it’s always during my opponent’s best serve.
Net gains? Only when I’m not at the net.
Tennis is therapy where your racket pays for the emotional outbursts.
Every serve begins with hope and ends with… error.
I don’t chase balls. I politely ask them to come to me—and then pretend it’s strategy.
Tennis teaches patience: if you wait long enough, your opponent will unforced-error themselves.
My backhand? Half skill, half ‘let’s see what happens.’
In tennis, love means nothing… until you still have feelings after losing.
Don’t hate the player. Hate the match points they keep saving.
My coaching advice: Hit the ball. If it lands in, claim it was luck. If it misses, pretend spin was the issue.
Tennis is the only sport where grunting louder can actually improve your chances.
My serve is like my dating life: sometimes hot, mostly fault.
I asked my partner to play mixed doubles—now we’re arguing over who’s on top at the net.
In tennis, size doesn’t matter… unless we’re talking about the balls.
He said my grip was too tight—then wondered why I broke his strings.
Love means nothing in tennis, but everything in the locker room.
She told me to take it slow, so I sliced instead of smashing.
A long rally is great foreplay, but eventually you’ve got to finish the point.
New balls, please—because the old ones can’t handle me anymore.
I don’t always win, but when I do, it’s because my opponent forgot how to play.
Tennis is like a relationship: it’s all about the right angles and avoiding the net.
I have no sense of humor about losing. — Rafael Nadal
Being number two sucks. — Andre Agassi
Experience is a great advantage. The problem is that when you get the experience, you’re too damned old to do anything about it. — Jimmy Connors
Tennis is a psychological sport, you have to keep a clear head. That is why I stopped playing. — Boris Becker
The fifth set is not about tennis; it’s about nerves. — Boris Becker
Tennis was never work for me, tennis was fun. And the tougher the battle and the longer the match, the more fun I had. — Jimmy Connors
I told myself to play free. You play the ball. You don’t play the opponent. Be free in your head. Be free in your shots. Go for it. The brave will be rewarded here. — Roger Federer
You have to believe in the long-term plan you have, but you need the short-term goals to motivate and inspire you. — Roger Federer
Whoever said ‘It’s not whether you win or lose that counts’ probably lost. — Martina Navratilova
The mark of great sportsmen is not how good they are at their best but how good they are at their worst. — Martina Navratilova
If you can keep playing tennis when somebody is shooting a gun down the street, that’s concentration. — Serena Williams
Tennis is just a game, family is forever. — Serena Williams
In tennis, it is not the opponent you fear, it is the failure itself, knowing how near you were but just out of reach. — Andy Murray
I have always considered tennis as a combat in an arena between two gladiators who have their racquets and their courage as their weapons. — Yannick Noah
Tennis is a perfect combination of violent action taking place in an atmosphere of total tranquility. — Billie Jean King
Losing is not my enemy, fear of losing is my enemy. — Rafael Nadal
You have to believe in yourself when no one else does — that makes you a winner right there. — Novak Djokovic
Tennis uses the language of life. Advantage, service, fault, break, love — the basic elements of tennis are those of everyday existence, because every match is a life in miniature. — Andre Agassi
Winning is great, but if you are really going to do something in life, the secret is learning how to lose. — Andre Agassi
If you can react the same way to winning and losing, that’s a big accomplishment. That quality is important because it stays with you the rest of your life. — Chris Evert
What rivalry? I win all the matches. — Martina Hingis
Tennis spoke to me. I liked being alone on the court. I liked the idea of all the mistakes being on me. — Andre Agassi
When I was 40, my doctor advised me that a man in his 40s shouldn’t play tennis. I heeded his advice carefully and could hardly wait until I reached 50 to start again. — Hugo Black
I play Cinderella tennis. That is, I don’t quite get to the ball. — Larry Adler
To be a great champion, you must believe you are the best. If you’re not, pretend you are. — John McEnroe
New balls, same old excuses.
Tennis: where love means nothing and everything at the same time.
